role_hints.role: Engineers (aerospace, mechanical, electrical, materials — the likely real core)
role_hints.oews_basis: 17-2141 Mechanical Engineers 1.9% listed; role_mix is the 5-digit 54171 proxy biased toward biotech — real 541715 firms (defense R&D, product development, applied physical science) carry aerospace/electrical/materials engineers in the unlisted tail, est. ~15-25% more
role_hints.note: Modeling, simulation setup, analysis, drawings and technical-report/proposal writing are heavily AI-exposed; hardware prototyping, test execution and classified/on-site work are not. Plausible within-role fraction 0.25-0.45.

role_hints.oews_basis: 19-1042 Medical Scientists 6.1% + 19-1021 Biochemists/Biophysicists 2.2% (both partly proxy artifacts from 541714's weight in 54171) + physicists/chemists in the tail, est. ~5-10% more
role_hints.note: Analysis and writing automate; lab experimentation and instrument work are physical.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-9121 Natural Sciences Managers 7.3% + 11-9199 Managers All Other 3.1%
role_hints.note: Program direction and sponsor relationships persist; reporting, resourcing and proposal assembly automate.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers 5.2% + 13-1082 Project Management Specialists 2.6% + 13-1199 Business Operations Specialists 1.6%
role_hints.note: Government contract administration, compliance and cost-accounting paperwork is a large, heavily automatable overhead in FAR-governed firms.

role_hints.oews_basis: 15-1252 Software Developers 6.6% + tail data/simulation staff est. ~2-3% more
role_hints.note: Simulation, embedded and analysis code; AI-augmented — and often the deliverable itself in defense R&D.

role_hints.oews_basis: 19-4021 Biological Technicians 1.5% listed (proxy artifact) + engineering technicians, machinists and test staff in the tail, est. ~8-12% more
role_hints.note: Prototype fabrication, test rigs, instrumentation and field trials — physical work; augmented but not replaced.

role_hints.oews_basis: not in listed top-10; 54171 top-10 covers only ~38% of wage bill — admin, contracts, security/facility-clearance and misc. staff
role_hints.note: Document and admin work automates; security-cleared facility staff and compliance officers persist by mandate.

source_hints.source: NSF Business Enterprise Research and Development (BERD) Survey
source_hints.why: Official business R&D composition and funding-source data — separates federal from company-funded R&D for this code's dominant-segment call.
Small applied-R&D firms here are heavily SBIR/STTR-dependent — award concentration evidences revenue model and customer concentration for the band population.
The relevant deal market for defense-funded R&D shops — active acquirers and multiples for exit_mult.

source_hints.source: GovWin / Deltek federal contracting market reports
source_hints.why: Federal services market structure, small-business set-aside dynamics and recompete data affecting acquirability of SBIR-dependent firms.

source_hints.source: FAR Part 31 / DCAA audit guidance (cost-reimbursable contracting)
source_hints.why: Primary documents for the cost-plus clawback mechanism — audited indirect rates pass AI productivity gains to the government.

source_hints.source: Census Business Trends and Outlook Survey (BTOS) AI supplement
source_hints.why: Official AI-use anchor for professional/scientific services in the absence of a dedicated survey.

capture_questions: Federal R&D work — likely the dominant funding source — is priced cost-plus-fixed-fee with DCAA-audited direct and indirect rates: when AI cuts labor hours, negotiated rates and cost-based fees hand the savings to the government. What share of the dominant sub-segment's revenue is cost-reimbursable federal vs fixed-price (SBIR, firm-fixed-price) vs commercial NRE, and who keeps AI savings under each?

Who owns the customer relationship — the firm's contract vehicles, facility clearances and past-performance record (institutional, survives acquisition) or individual principal investigators whose expertise and clearances are personally portable?

Small-business status is itself the moat for SBIR/set-aside revenue — does acquisition by a roll-up platform destroy eligibility (size-standard recertification), gutting the acquired revenue base? This is a structural capture question specific to this code.
What stops sponsors from insourcing the analytical/simulation portion with AI, leaving only physical test and classified-facility work outsourced?
terminal_value_question: The mechanisms holding paid demand are physical test infrastructure, security clearances/classified facilities, and statutory small-business R&D set-asides — none of which AI replaces directly. The deciding question: does the dominant sub-segment's revenue sit in those protected slices, or in analysis/simulation/documentation hours that mature AI (in the sponsor's hands) replaces — and do federal buyers recalibrate cost-based contract values downward as AI-assisted productivity becomes the audited norm? Distinguish operator cost-cutting from product replacement, and note that for cost-plus revenue, operator cost-cutting is itself revenue shrinkage.
special_notes: HETEROGENEOUS R&D CODE: 541715 spans defense/aerospace R&D contractors, commercial product-development and engineering-research firms, agricultural and materials research labs, and grant-funded ventures. Identify the top-3 revenue sub-segments, score the DOMINANT one, and set heterogeneous: true. Best hypothesis, flagged as hypothesis: federally funded applied R&D (defense/energy/space, incl. SBIR-dependent shops) is the dominant sub-segment, with commercial contract product-development second.

Pricing-model deflation candidate (central): cost-plus/cost-reimbursable federal contracts with DCAA-audited rates convert AI efficiency into government savings — model in pi_dist explicitly; fixed-price SBIR awards are capture-friendlier but capped and non-scalable.

Roll-up-specific trap: SBIR/STTR and small-business set-aside revenue can evaporate on acquisition (SBA size-standard affiliation rules) — a consolidator buying five SBIR-dependent shops may destroy the revenue it bought; probe this before scoring B and pi_moat.
Role-mix caveat: the 54171 proxy over-weights biotech occupations (medical scientists, biological technicians); rebuild roles toward engineers, physicists and test staff for this code and say so.

n_band note: 1,716 firms with 500+ class firms (549) top-heavy — the code contains both micro shops and giant institutes (Battelle-scale); the $1-10M band population is real but sits mostly in the 20-99 employee class (1,197 firms per the record's chain).

Boundary confusion: 541714 (biotech), 541713 (nano), 541330 (engineering services — fee-for-design vs R&D distinction is blurry for product-development firms), 541380 (testing labs), plus FFRDCs/UARCs that distort any market-size statistics; check what a source counts before quoting.
